editorial_thesis: Agent capability is becoming portable and deployable, so the differentiator is the control plane: compatibility tests, scoped authority, explainable actions, and rollout evidence.

topic_groups.title: MCP Portability + Agent Extensions
topic_groups.selection_rationale: The protocol’s July 28 stateless transition and fresh package-based extension surfaces make portable, versioned agent capability a timely practical seam.

sources.title: GitHub MCP Server supports the next MCP specification
sources.why_selected: GitHub has shipped ahead of MCP’s July 28 stateless-core transition; sessions and initialize disappear while backwards compatibility remains.
sources.usefulness: Use it as a compatibility deadline for any Buildn/client MCP client or server; run the conformance suite before changing production tooling.

sources.title: Extend eve agents with installable extensions
sources.why_selected: Eve packages tools, connections, skills, instructions, and hooks as installable, versioned extensions with config schemas.
sources.usefulness: Model Buildn’s reusable client connectors as dependency-like bundles: CRM lookup, reporting, or scoped publishing with an audit hook.

sources.title: GitHub tools are now an installable eve extension
sources.why_selected: A concrete extension ships 42 GitHub tools with connector-backed short-lived tokens, presets, and per-write approval rules.
sources.usefulness: Useful reference architecture for client-repo triage or code-review agents: scope a preset, mint runtime credentials, gate external comments.

topic_groups.title: Governed Agent Delivery
topic_groups.selection_rationale: Fresh GitHub and Vercel changes expose a useful control-plane pattern: proposal rationale, approval posture, deployment, rollout telemetry, and reversible configuration.

sources.title: Agent automation controls in GitHub Issues
sources.why_selected: Issue agents can attach rationale and confidence, suggest lower-confidence metadata changes, and support review queues via has:suggestions.
sources.usefulness: Use it for bounded issue triage: labels, types, fields, assignment, and closure—with human review for uncertain cases.

sources.title: Vercel MCP can now deploy code
sources.why_selected: The deploy_to_vercel MCP tool can create or target a project, detect a framework, install, build, and return a shareable URL.
sources.usefulness: Useful for disposable preview deployments from an agent-produced artifact, especially client review loops.

sources.title: Evaluation metrics for Vercel Flags
sources.why_selected: Flag detail pages now show evaluations per minute and filters for variant, reason, environment, SDK key, client, and project.
sources.usefulness: Turn an agent rollout into observable delivery: confirm the intended audience receives the intended variant before expanding exposure.

sources.title: Inspect feature flag history with Vercel CLI
sources.why_selected: vercel flags versions and semantic diffs expose authorship, environments, targeting rules, rollout percentages, and condition changes.
sources.usefulness: Use the CLI output as an evidence attachment in a release note or incident review.

topic_groups.title: Physical AI + Generative Simulation
topic_groups.selection_rationale: This is a deliberately under-covered lane: two July 23 research releases show reusable architecture patterns for dynamic-world creation and coordinating many local actors.

sources.title: GS-Agent: Creating 4D Physical Worlds With Generative Simulation
sources.why_selected: GS-Agent coordinates specialized agents that write code against a physics engine and use multimodal feedback to iteratively build controllable 4D scenes.
sources.usefulness: A useful pattern for Buildn creative work: prompt → scene plan → executable simulation/render → visual review → revision, rather than one-shot video generation.

sources.title: Compact Latent Coordination for Autonomous Vehicles at Unsignalized Intersections
sources.why_selected: MAPS separates centralized strategic intent (a compact proto-plan) from decentralized local execution; the paper reports zero-shot scaling from three to five agents in simulation.
sources.usefulness: A conceptual design cue for Buildn workflows: let a coordinator publish a compact plan while specialist agents keep local context and bounded actions.
